machine: Apple Silicon (Mac), Python 3.12, PyTorch CPU build
notes: Krunch reference implementation (vendor/Krunch) run through scripts/compressor.py. Single-stream, device=cpu, segment_length=2048. Timed end-to-end via subprocess wall time including Python startup and checkpoint loading.

results.roundtrip_note: decompression path not exercised yet; ratio numbers from scripts/compressor.py direct output
decompression path not exercised yet; ratio numbers from scripts/compressor.py direct output

key_finding: Krunch-200K (200k params) and Krunch-3.2M (3.2M params) have 16x parameter and ~11x compute difference but only 1.23x runtime difference. This means framework overhead dominates runtime (~97% for the 200K model) and Rust/ggml port should achieve 5-10x single-stream speedup purely by eliminating Python/PyTorch op dispatch overhead.
